// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// MODULE: Memory Tools
// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Dispatch for L2-L4 memory tools: search, triggers, save,
// List, stats, health, delete, update, validate (T303).
import {
  handleMemorySearch,
  handleMemoryMatchTriggers,
  handleMemorySave,
  handleMemoryList,
  handleMemoryStats,
  handleMemoryHealth,
  handleMemoryDelete,
  handleMemoryUpdate,
  handleMemoryValidate,
  handleMemoryBulkDelete,
} from '../handlers/index.js';
import { validateToolArgs } from '../schemas/tool-input-schemas.js';

import { parseArgs } from './types.js';
import type {
  MCPResponse,
  SearchArgs,
  TriggerArgs,
  SaveArgs,
  ListArgs,
  StatsArgs,
  HealthArgs,
  DeleteArgs,
  UpdateArgs,
  MemoryValidateArgs,
  BulkDeleteArgs,
} from './types.js';

function relabelResponseTool(response: MCPResponse, toolName: string): MCPResponse {
  const firstEntry = response?.content?.[0];
  if (!firstEntry || firstEntry.type !== 'text' || typeof firstEntry.text !== 'string') {
    return response;
  }

  try {
    const envelope = JSON.parse(firstEntry.text) as Record<string, unknown>;
    const meta = envelope.meta && typeof envelope.meta === 'object'
      ? envelope.meta as Record<string, unknown>
      : {};
    envelope.meta = {
      ...meta,
      tool: toolName,
    };

    return {
      ...response,
      content: [{ ...firstEntry, text: JSON.stringify(envelope, null, 2) }],
    };
  } catch {
    return response;
  }
}

/** Tool names handled by this module */
export const TOOL_NAMES = new Set([
  'memory_search',
  'memory_quick_search',
  'memory_match_triggers',
  'memory_save',
  'memory_list',
  'memory_stats',
  'memory_health',
  'memory_delete',
  'memory_update',
  'memory_validate',
  'memory_bulk_delete',
]);

/** Dispatch a tool call. Returns null if tool name not handled. */
export async function handleTool(name: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<MCPResponse | null> {
  switch (name) {
    case 'memory_search':         return handleMemorySearch(parseArgs<SearchArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_search', args)));
    case 'memory_quick_search': {
      // E3: Delegate to memory_search with sensible defaults
      const validated = validateToolArgs('memory_quick_search', args);
      const quickArgs: SearchArgs = {
        query: validated.query as string,
        limit: typeof validated.limit === 'number' ? validated.limit : 10,
        specFolder: validated.specFolder as string | undefined,
        tenantId: validated.tenantId as string | undefined,
        userId: validated.userId as string | undefined,
        agentId: validated.agentId as string | undefined,
        autoDetectIntent: true,
        enableDedup: true,
        includeContent: true,
        includeConstitutional: true,
        rerank: true,
      };
      const response = await handleMemorySearch(quickArgs);
      return relabelResponseTool(response, 'memory_quick_search');
    }
    case 'memory_match_triggers': return handleMemoryMatchTriggers(parseArgs<TriggerArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_match_triggers', args)));
    case 'memory_save':           return handleMemorySave(parseArgs<SaveArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_save', args)));
    case 'memory_list':           return handleMemoryList(parseArgs<ListArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_list', args)));
    case 'memory_stats':          return handleMemoryStats(parseArgs<StatsArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_stats', args)));
    case 'memory_health':         return handleMemoryHealth(parseArgs<HealthArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_health', args)));
    case 'memory_delete':         return handleMemoryDelete(parseArgs<DeleteArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_delete', args)));
    case 'memory_update':         return handleMemoryUpdate(parseArgs<UpdateArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_update', args)));
    case 'memory_validate':       return handleMemoryValidate(parseArgs<MemoryValidateArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_validate', args)));
    case 'memory_bulk_delete':    return handleMemoryBulkDelete(parseArgs<BulkDeleteArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_bulk_delete', args)));
    default: return null;
  }
}

/**
 * Internal search result row — enriched DB row used within this handler.
 * NOT the same as the canonical SearchResult in shared/types.ts.
 * Self-contained: uses local types instead of the deprecated MemoryRow/MemoryRecord shapes.
 * This can migrate to MemoryDbRow & Record<string, unknown> later without changing the handler contract.
 */
interface MemorySearchRow extends Record<string, unknown> {
  id: number;
  similarity?: number;
  importance_tier?: string;
  contextType?: string;
  context_type?: string;
  attentionScore?: number;
  retrievability?: number;
  stability?: number;
  last_review?: string | null;
  created_at?: string;
  last_accessed?: number;
  content?: string;
  memoryState?: string;
  file_path?: string;
  parent_id?: number | null;
  chunk_index?: number | null;
  chunk_label?: string | null;
  isChunk?: boolean;
  parentId?: number | null;
  chunkIndex?: number | null;
  chunkLabel?: string | null;
  chunkCount?: number | null;
  contentSource?: 'reassembled_chunks' | 'file_read_fallback';
  precomputedContent?: string;
}

interface DedupResult {

  'spec',

function normalizeDocumentType(value: unknown): string | null {
  if (typeof value !== 'string') {
    return null;
  }
  const normalized = value.trim().toLowerCase();
  return normalized.length > 0 ? normalized : null;
}

function resolveAnchorId(row: Record<string, unknown>): string | null {
  const value = row.anchor_id ?? row.anchorId;
  return typeof value === 'string' && value.trim().length > 0
    ? value.trim()
    : null;
}

function resolveFilePath(row: Record<string, unknown>): string | null {
  const value = row.file_path ?? row.filePath;
  return typeof value === 'string' && value.trim().length > 0
    ? value
    : null;
}

function resolveCanonicalSourceKind(row: Record<string, unknown>): CanonicalSourceKind | null {
  const documentType = normalizeDocumentType(row.document_type ?? row.documentType);
  const importanceTier = normalizeDocumentType(row.importance_tier ?? row.importanceTier);
  const anchorId = resolveAnchorId(row);

  if (importanceTier === 'constitutional' || documentType === 'constitutional') {

     8	Spec Kit Memory MCP tools, behavior notes, and configuration options.
     9	
    10	Current baseline: schema v23 (`document_type`, `spec_level`), 3 indexed content sources, 7 intent types, and `includeSpecDocs: true` by default.
    11	
    12	---
    13	
    14	<!-- ANCHOR:overview -->
    15	## 1. OVERVIEW
    16	
    17	The Spec Kit Memory system provides context preservation across sessions through vector-based semantic search and packet-first continuity. Phase 018 makes `handover.md -> _memory.continuity -> spec docs` the canonical recovery chain; retired `[spec]/memory/*.md` artifacts are no longer produced at save time and only matter when older packets still contain them. This reference covers MCP tool behavior, importance tiers, decay scoring, and configuration.
    18	
    19	When a save mutates indexed state, the runtime also updates the `DB_UPDATED_FILE` marker from `mcp_server/core/config.ts`. Long-lived MCP processes poll that marker with `checkDatabaseUpdated()` so canonical-doc writes become visible without restarting the server.
    20	
    21	### Architecture
    22	
    23	| Component | Location | Purpose |
    24	|-----------|----------|---------|
    25	| MCP Server | `mcp_server/context-server.ts` | Spec Kit Memory MCP with vector search |
    26	| Database | `mcp_server/dist/database/context-index.sqlite` | SQLite with FTS5 + vector embeddings (canonical runtime path; `mcp_server/database/context-index.sqlite` is a compatibility symlink) |
    27	| Constitutional | `constitutional/` | Always-surface rules (Gate 3 enforcement) |
    28	| Scripts | runtime `scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js` (source: `scripts/memory/generate-context.ts`) | Canonical continuity save entrypoint for packet docs |
    29	
    30	### Core Capabilities
    31	
    32	- **Semantic search** - Find indexed spec-doc records by meaning, not just keywords
    33	- **Importance tiers** - Six-level system for prioritizing context
    34	- **Decay scoring** - Recent indexed spec-doc records rank higher than older ones
    35	- **Checkpoints** - Save/restore indexed-continuity state snapshots
    36	- **Constitutional tier** - Critical rules that always surface
    37	
    38	### Indexable Content Sources
    39	
    40	The indexed-continuity store indexes content from two active sources, plus a retired-compatibility row preserved for read-side retrieval against historical packets:
    41	
    42	| Source | Location Pattern | Memory Type | Default Tier | Discovery |
    43	|--------|-----------------|-------------|--------------|-----------|
    44	| **Spec Documents** | `specs/**/*.md` and `.opencode/specs/**/*.md` | Per-type (spec, plan, tasks, etc.) | `normal` | `findSpecDocuments()` |
    45	| **Constitutional Rules** | `.opencode/skill/*/constitutional/*.md` | `meta-cognitive` | `constitutional` | `findConstitutionalFiles()` |
    46	| **Retired Compatibility Artifacts** | Older `specs/*/memory/*.{md,txt}` files already present in historical packets | Varies (episodic, procedural, etc.) | `normal` | Historical compatibility only |
    47	
    48	**Content Source Behavior:**
    49	
    50	- **Spec Documents** — Canonical packet continuity source. Recovery should read `handover.md`, then `_memory.continuity`, then the rest of the packet docs before widening into search.
    51	- **Constitutional Rules** — Always-surface critical rules. Injected at top of every search result. No decay.
    52	- **Retired Compatibility Artifacts** — Older session notes may still exist in historical packets, but save workflows no longer produce them and operators should not treat them as an active surface.
    53	
    54	**Spec Document Indexing Pipeline:**
    55	1. `findSpecDocuments()` walks both supported specs roots and discovers supported doc filenames
    56	2. `isMemoryFile()` validates each document as an indexable file
    57	3. `extractDocumentType()` infers `document_type` (spec/plan/tasks/etc.) for scoring
    58	4. Documents are indexed alongside memory and constitutional files
    59	5. `createSpecDocumentChain()` links lifecycle docs for causal traversal
    60	
    61	---
    62	
    63	<!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->
    64	<!-- ANCHOR:importance-tiers -->
    65	## 2. IMPORTANCE TIERS
    66	
    67	Six-tier system for prioritizing memory relevance:
    68	
    69	| Tier | Weight | searchBoost | Purpose | Auto-Surface |
    70	|------|--------|-------------|---------|--------------|
    71	| **Constitutional** | 1.0 | 3.0 | Critical rules that ALWAYS apply | Yes (top of every search) |
    72	| **Critical** | 1.0 | 2.0 | High-importance context | Yes (high relevance) |
    73	| **Important** | 0.8 | 1.5 | Significant decisions/context | Relevance-based |
    74	| **Normal** | 0.5 | 1.0 | Standard session context | Relevance-based |
    75	| **Temporary** | 0.3 | 0.5 | Short-term notes | Relevance-based |
    76	| **Deprecated** | 0.1 | 0.0 | Outdated (kept for history) | Never (excluded from search) |
    77	
    78	**searchBoost Multipliers:** Applied to search scores to prioritize higher tiers:
    79	- Constitutional memories get 3x boost in search ranking
    80	- Critical memories get 2x boost
    81	- Important memories get 1.5x boost
    82	- Normal memories have no boost (1.0x)
    83	- Temporary and Deprecated get reduced visibility (0.5x and 0x respectively)
    84	
    85	### Constitutional Tier Behavior
    86	
    87	- Stored in `constitutional/` folder
    88	- Auto-indexed on MCP server startup
    89	- **ALWAYS** surface at top of search results, regardless of query
    90	- Used for gate enforcement (e.g., "always ask spec folder question")
    91	- EXEMPT from decay scoring (always max relevance)
    92	
    93	---
    94	
    95	<!-- /ANCHOR:importance-tiers -->

    97	## 3. MCP TOOLS
    98	
    99	> **Note:** MCP tool names use plain names such as `memory_search`, `memory_save`, and `checkpoint_create`.
   100	
   101	### Tool Reference (43 tools)
   102	
   103	| Layer | Tool | Purpose | Example Use |
   104	|-------|------|---------|-------------|
   105	| L1: Orchestration | `memory_context()` | Unified entry point with intent-aware routing (7 intents) | START HERE for most memory operations |
   106	| L1: Orchestration | `session_resume()` | Resume memory, code graph, and CocoIndex state in one call | Detailed recovery payload after reconnect, or when you want direct merged resume state |
   107	| L1: Orchestration | `session_bootstrap()` | Composite bootstrap combining resume and health checks | Canonical first tool call in a fresh OpenCode-style session or after `/clear` |
   108	| L2: Core | `memory_search()` | Semantic search with vector similarity | Find prior decisions on auth |
   109	| L2: Core | `memory_quick_search()` | Simplified search wrapper for fast lookups | Quick keyword-based retrieval |
   110	| L2: Core | `memory_match_triggers()` | Fast keyword matching (<50ms) with cognitive features | Gate enforcement |
   111	| L2: Core | `memory_save()` | Index a saved continuity artifact or spec doc. Re-generates embedding when **content hash** changes. Title-only changes do not trigger re-embedding. | After generate-context.js |
   112	| L3: Discovery | `memory_list()` | Browse stored spec-doc records with pagination (parent rows by default) | Review session history |
   113	| L3: Discovery | `memory_stats()` | Get memory system statistics with composite scoring | Check index health |
   114	| L3: Discovery | `memory_health()` | Check health status of memory system | Diagnose issues |
   115	| L3: Discovery | `session_health()` | Report session readiness, graph freshness, and priming status | Detect stale session context before continuing |
   116	| L4: Mutation | `memory_delete()` | Delete memory by ID or bulk delete by spec folder | Remove outdated memories |
   117	| L4: Mutation | `memory_update()` | Update memory metadata (title, tier, triggers) | Correct memory properties |
   118	| L4: Mutation | `memory_validate()` | Mark memory as useful/not useful | Confidence scoring |
   119	| L4: Mutation | `memory_bulk_delete()` | Bulk delete memories by spec folder with confirmation | Clean up entire spec folder memories |
   120	| L5: Lifecycle | `checkpoint_create()` | Save named state snapshot | Before risky changes |
   121	| L5: Lifecycle | `checkpoint_list()` | List available checkpoints | Find restore points |
   122	| L5: Lifecycle | `checkpoint_restore()` | Restore from checkpoint | Rollback if needed |
   123	| L5: Lifecycle | `checkpoint_delete()` | Delete a checkpoint | Clean up old snapshots |
   124	| L6: Analysis | `task_preflight()` | Capture epistemic baseline before task execution | Start of implementation work |
   125	| L6: Analysis | `task_postflight()` | Capture epistemic state after task, calculate Learning Index | After completing implementation |
   126	| L6: Analysis | `memory_drift_why()` | Trace causal chain for a spec-doc record ("why was this decided?") | Understand decision lineage |
   127	| L6: Analysis | `memory_causal_link()` | Create causal relationship between two memories | Link decision to its cause |
   128	| L6: Analysis | `memory_causal_stats()` | Get statistics about the causal memory graph | Check causal coverage |
   129	| L6: Analysis | `memory_causal_unlink()` | Remove a causal relationship by edge ID | Clean up incorrect links |
   130	| L6: Analysis | `eval_run_ablation()` | Run ablation study on memory scoring components | Compare scoring strategies |
   131	| L6: Analysis | `eval_reporting_dashboard()` | Generate evaluation and reporting dashboard data | Review system metrics |
   132	| L6: Analysis | `code_graph_query()` | Query structural relationships such as callers, imports, and outlines | Find what calls a symbol or which files import a module |
   133	| L6: Analysis | `code_graph_context()` | Expand CocoIndex or symbol seeds into compact graph neighborhoods | Pull structural context for an LLM prompt |
   134	| L7: Maintenance | `memory_index_scan()` | Bulk scan and index packet docs, constitutional files, and graph metadata | After continuity or spec-doc updates |
   135	| L7: Maintenance | `memory_ingest_start()` | Start async bulk memory ingestion | Import large memory sets |
   136	| L7: Maintenance | `memory_ingest_status()` | Check status of running ingestion job | Monitor import progress |
   137	| L7: Maintenance | `memory_ingest_cancel()` | Cancel a running ingestion job | Stop runaway imports |
   138	| L7: Maintenance | `memory_get_learning_history()` | Get learning history (preflight/postflight records) | Analyze learning patterns |
   139	| L7: Maintenance | `code_graph_scan()` | Build or refresh the structural code graph index | Re-index after branch switches or large code changes |
   140	| L7: Maintenance | `code_graph_status()` | Report code graph freshness and node/edge counts | Check whether the structural index is usable |

 succeeded in 0ms:
     8	Complete guide to saving conversation context, execution methods, and retrieval.
     9	
    10	---
    11	
    12	<!-- ANCHOR:overview -->
    13	## 1. OVERVIEW
    14	
    15	### Core Principle
    16	
    17	Execute save operations through whichever method fits your workflow - slash commands for convenience, direct scripts for control. All paths feed the same canonical save entrypoint, update the target packet's continuity surfaces, and reindex the resulting packet docs for retrieval.
    18	
    19	When direct CLI mode includes an explicit spec-folder argument, that target is authoritative. Session-learning matches, JSON `SPEC_FOLDER` fields, and auto-detect may inform diagnostics, but they must not reroute the save to another folder.
    20	
    21	Direct phase-folder targets are supported. If the explicit CLI target resolves to a policy-defined phase folder, `generate-context.js` preserves that target and updates the selected phase folder's canonical continuity surfaces instead of rerouting elsewhere.
    22	
    23	### Governed Save Boundaries
    24	
    25	The save workflow stays compatible with private and agent-scoped operation. When the surrounding runtime enables governance, save and follow-up retrieval flows may carry `tenantId`, `userId`, and `agentId` so memory capture stays inside the intended boundary.
    26	
    27	### Execution Paths
    28	
    29	The indexed-continuity store supports **2 independent execution paths**. Any method can be used standalone.
    30	
    31	### Method Comparison
    32	
    33	| Method            | AI Agent Required | Best For                           | Effort | Token Cost |
    34	| ----------------- | ----------------- | ---------------------------------- | ------ | ---------- |
    35	| **Slash Command** | Yes               | Interactive saves, manual triggers | Low    | ~200       |
    36	| **Direct Script** | No                | Testing, debugging, CI/CD          | Medium | 0          |
    37	
    38	### Execution Flow
    39	
    40	```
    41	┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    42	│                    MEMORY SAVE PATHWAYS                        │
    43	├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    44	│                                                                │
    45	│   Slash Command                    Direct Script               │
    46	│   ┌──────────┐                    ┌──────────┐                 │
    47	│   │ /memory: │                    │  node    │                 │
    48	│   │  save    │                    │ script.ts│                 │
    49	│   └────┬─────┘                    └────┬─────┘                 │
    50	│        │                               │                       │
    51	│        ▼                               ▼                       │
    52	│   ┌──────────┐                    ┌──────────┐                 │
    53	│   │ AI Agent │                    │   JSON   │                 │
    54	│   │ Analysis │                    │   Input  │                 │
    55	│   └────┬─────┘                    └────┬─────┘                 │
    56	│        │                               │                       │
    57	│        └───────────────────────────────┘                       │
    58	│                              │                                 │
    59	│                              ▼                                 │
    60	│                    ┌─────────────────┐                         │
    61	│                    │ generate-context│                         │
    62	│                    │      .ts        │                         │
    63	│                    └────────┬────────┘                         │
    64	│                             │                                  │
    65	│                             ▼                                  │
    66	│                    ┌─────────────────┐                         │
    67	│                    │ canonical packet│                         │
    68	│                    │ continuity docs │                         │
    69	│                    └─────────────────┘                         │
    70	│                                                                │
    71	└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    72	```
    73	
    74	---
    75	
    76	<!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->
    77	<!-- ANCHOR:method-selection -->
    78	## 2. METHOD SELECTION
    79	
    80	### Decision Tree
    81	
    82	```
    83	┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    84	│              WHICH METHOD SHOULD I USE?                         │
    85	└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    86	                              │
    87	                              ▼
    88	              ┌───────────────────────────────┐
    89	              │ Is an AI agent active in      │
    90	              │ the current conversation?     │
    91	              └───────────────┬───────────────┘
    92	                              │
    93	               ┌──────────────┴──────────────┐
    94	               │                             │
    95	              YES                           NO
    96	               │                             │
    97	               ▼                             ▼
    98	┌─────────────────────────┐   ┌─────────────────────────┐
    99	│ Need automatic          │   │ Prepare JSON, then use  │
   100	│ conversation analysis?  │   │ Direct Script method    │
   101	└────────────┬────────────┘   └─────────────────────────┘
   102	             │
   103	      ┌──────┴──────┐
   104	      │             │
   105	     YES           NO
   106	      │             │
   107	      ▼             ▼
   108	┌───────────┐ ┌───────────┐
   109	│   SLASH   │ │  DIRECT   │
   110	│  COMMAND  │ │  SCRIPT   │
   111	│           │ │ (custom   │
   112	│ /memory:  │ │  JSON)    │
   113	│   save    │ │           │
   114	└───────────┘ └───────────┘
   115	```
   116	
   117	### Quick Selection Guide
   118	
   119	| Scenario                                  | Recommended Method |
   120	| ----------------------------------------- | ------------------ |
   121	| End of work session, want AI to summarize | **Slash Command**  |
   122	| CI/CD pipeline, automated saves           | **Direct Script**  |
   123	| Quick manual save, no AI available        | **Direct Script**  |
   124	| Testing memory system functionality       | **Direct Script**  |
   125	| Batch processing multiple saves           | **Direct Script**  |
   126	| Interactive session with full context     | **Slash Command**  |
   127	
   128	---
   129	
   130	<!-- /ANCHOR:method-selection -->
   131	<!-- ANCHOR:slash-commands -->
   132	## 3. SLASH COMMANDS
   133	
   134	**When to Use:** Manual save with AI-powered conversation analysis
   135	**Requirement:** Slash command files exist in `.opencode/command/memory/`
   136	
   137	### Available Commands
   138	
   139	```
   140	/memory:save       # Save current conversation context
   141	/memory:search  # Unified retrieval + analysis: search, epistemic baselines, causal graph, evaluation
   142	```
   143	
   144	### Execution Flow
   145	
   146	1. Slash command expands to full prompt
   147	2. AI agent analyzes conversation history
   148	3. AI agent creates structured JSON summary (the main agent or a delegated packet writer can invoke generate-context.js for memory under distributed governance)
   149	4. AI agent calls `generate-context.js` with JSON data
   150	5. Canonical continuity updated inside the active root-spec or phase packet
   151	
   152	### Validation Checkpoints
   153	
   154	| Checkpoint       | Verification                   | Action on Failure         |
   155	| ---------------- | ------------------------------ | ------------------------- |
   156	| Command exists   | `ls .opencode/command/memory/` | Create command file       |
   157	| AI agent active  | Check response capability      | Use Direct Script instead |
   158	| Spec folder arg  | Passed via CLI argument        | Specify folder manually   |
   159	| Write permission | `test -w specs/###/`           | Check packet permissions  |
   160	
   161	### Example Output
   162	
   163	```
   164	✓ Context analyzed (12 exchanges detected)
   165	✓ Spec folder: 049-anchor-context-retrieval
   166	✓ Continuity surfaces updated for: 049-anchor-context-retrieval
   167	✓ Primary continuity block: implementation-summary.md::_memory.continuity
   168	✓ Packet docs reindexed
   169	```
   170	
   171	---
   172	
   173	<!-- /ANCHOR:slash-commands -->
   174	<!-- ANCHOR:direct-script -->
   175	## 4. DIRECT SCRIPT
   176	
   177	**When to Use:** Testing, debugging, custom workflows, CI/CD pipelines
   178	**Requirement:** Node.js installed
   179	
   180	### Usage Pattern
   181	
   182	```bash
   183	# Create minimal JSON data file
   184	cat > /tmp/test-save-context.json << 'EOF'
   185	{
   186	  "SPEC_FOLDER": "049-anchor-context-retrieval",
   187	  "recent_context": [{
   188	    "request": "Test save-context execution",
   189	    "completed": "Verified system works standalone",
   190	    "learning": "Direct script execution requires minimal JSON",
   191	    "duration": "5m",
   192	    "date": "2025-11-28T18:30:00Z"
   193	  }],
   194	  "observations": [{
   195	    "type": "discovery",
   196	    "title": "Standalone execution test",
   197	    "narrative": "Testing direct script invocation",
   198	    "timestamp": "2025-11-28T18:30:00Z",
   199	    "files": [],
   200	    "facts": ["Standalone execution works", "Minimal data sufficient"]
   201	  }],
   202	  "user_prompts": [{
   203	    "prompt": "Test save-context standalone",
   204	    "timestamp": "2025-11-28T18:30:00Z"
   205	  }]
   206	}
   207	EOF
   208	
   209	# Execute script directly
   210	node .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js \

 succeeded in 0ms:
     8	Complete configuration guide for memory trigger phrases and the fast trigger matching system.
     9	
    10	---
    11	
    12	<!-- ANCHOR:overview -->
    13	## 1. OVERVIEW
    14	
    15	**Core Principle:** Trigger detection must be fast (<50ms) and reliable, using optimized phrase matching to surface relevant spec-doc records without impacting conversation flow.
    16	
    17	The spec-doc record workflow supports manual activation mechanisms:
    18	1. **Command Trigger** - `/memory:save` command for explicit saves
    19	2. **Phrase Triggers** - User phrases that directly invoke memory operations
    20	
    21	> **OpenCode Note:** Automatic interval-based saves (e.g., "every 20 messages") are NOT supported in OpenCode because OpenCode lacks the hooks system required to count messages and trigger saves automatically. All context preservation must be manually triggered.
    22	
    23	This reference covers trigger phrase configuration, the MCP-based matching system, and best practices for custom trigger design.
    24	
    25	### Key Components
    26	
    27	| Component | Purpose | Performance Target |
    28	|-----------|---------|-------------------|
    29	| `/memory:save` Command | Primary save trigger | Immediate |
    30	| Trigger Phrases | Explicit memory activation | <50ms detection |
    31	| MCP Tool | Fast phrase matching | <50ms response |
    32	| Custom Config | Project-specific triggers | Configurable |
    33	
    34	---
    35	
    36	<!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->
    37	<!-- ANCHOR:trigger-phrases -->
    38	## 2. TRIGGER PHRASES
    39	
    40	The following phrases activate memory operations (case-insensitive matching):
    41	
    42	### Primary Triggers
    43	
    44	| Category | Primary Phrase | Alternatives |
    45	|----------|----------------|--------------|
    46	| **Save** | "save context" | "save conversation", "save session" |
    47	| **Document** | "document this" | "preserve context", "save this discussion" |
    48	| **Remember** | "remember this" | "store this", "keep this context" |
    49	| **Checkpoint** | "checkpoint" | "save checkpoint", "create checkpoint" |
    50	
    51	### Detection Logic
    52	
    53	```typescript
    54	const TRIGGER_PHRASES: readonly string[] = [
    55	  // Save category
    56	  'save context',
    57	  'save conversation',
    58	  'save session',
    59	  'save this discussion',
    60	
    61	  // Document category
    62	  'document this',
    63	  'preserve context',
    64	
    65	  // Remember category
    66	  'remember this',
    67	  'store this',
    68	  'keep this context',
    69	
    70	  // Checkpoint category
    71	  'checkpoint',
    72	  'save checkpoint',
    73	  'create checkpoint'
    74	] as const;
    75	
    76	function detectTrigger(userMessage: string): boolean {
    77	  const normalized = userMessage.toLowerCase();
    78	  return TRIGGER_PHRASES.some(phrase => normalized.includes(phrase));
    79	}
    80	```
    81	
    82	### MCP Tool Integration
    83	
    84	The `memory_match_triggers` MCP tool provides fast trigger phrase matching without requiring embeddings:
    85	
    86	```typescript
    87	// Fast trigger matching (<50ms) - no embeddings required
    88	const result = await spec_kit_memory_memory_match_triggers({
    89	  prompt: "I want to save context for this session",
    90	  limit: 3  // Maximum matching spec-doc records to return
    91	});
    92	
    93	// Returns matching spec-doc records based on trigger phrases
    94	// Ideal for proactive memory surfacing during conversation
    95	```
    96	
    97	**Usage Scenarios:**
    98	- Quick keyword-based memory lookup before semantic search
    99	- Proactive memory surfacing during conversation
   100	- Fallback when semantic search is unavailable
   101	
   102	### Gate 3 Enforcement Triggers
   103	
   104	The constitutional memory for Gate 3 enforcement uses 33 trigger phrases to detect file modification intent:
   105	
   106	| Category | Trigger Phrases |
   107	|----------|-----------------|
   108	| **Create** | `create`, `add`, `generate`, `build`, `implement`, `write` |
   109	| **Modify** | `modify`, `edit`, `update`, `change`, `refactor`, `fix` |
   110	| **Delete** | `delete`, `remove`, `cleanup` |
   111	| **Move** | `rename`, `move`, `migrate` |
   112	| **Scale Indicators** | `comprehensive`, `all bugs`, `multiple files`, `codebase`, `entire`, `full`, `everything` |
   113	| **Agent Patterns** | `parallel agents`, `15 agents`, `10 agents`, `dispatch agents`, `opus agents` |
   114	| **Compound Actions** | `analyze and fix`, `find and fix`, `fix all`, `update all`, `modify all`, `check and fix` |
   115	| **Gate Keywords** | `spec folder`, `gate 3`, `file modification` |
   116	
   117	**How Gate 3 Trigger Matching Works:**
   118	
   119	1. AI calls `memory_match_triggers({ prompt: "user message" })`
   120	2. If prompt matches any Gate 3 trigger, constitutional memory surfaces
   121	3. AI sees reminder to ask spec folder question before file modifications
   122	
   123	**Example:**
   124	
   125	```typescript
   126	// User says: "refactor the authentication module"
   127	const matches = await memory_match_triggers({
   128	  prompt: "refactor the authentication module"
   129	});
   130	// Returns: Gate 3 enforcement constitutional memory 
   131	// matchedPhrases: ["refactor"]
   132	// AI then asks: "Spec Folder (required): A) Existing | B) New | C) Update related | D) Skip"
   133	```
   134	
   135	**Trigger Design Guidelines for Constitutional Memories:**
   136	
   137	| Guideline | Description |
   138	|-----------|-------------|
   139	| **Cover action verbs** | Include all verbs that indicate file modification intent |
   140	| **Include scale words** | Words like "comprehensive", "all", "entire" suggest large changes |
   141	| **Add domain terms** | Include project-specific terms for your enforcement use case |
   142	| **Test coverage** | Verify triggers match common user phrases |
   143	| **Limit count** | 20-40 triggers recommended for constitutional memories |
   144	
   145	---
   146	
   147	### Signal Vocabulary Expansion
   148	
   149	Beyond the default trigger phrases, the system recognizes expanded signal types for contextual trigger matching:
   150	
   151	| Signal | Purpose | Example Phrases |
   152	|--------|---------|----------------|
   153	| `CORRECTION` | Tracks when a spec-doc record is corrected or superseded | "actually", "correction", "that was wrong", "update previous" |
   154	| `PREFERENCE` | Captures user preference signals | "I prefer", "use this instead", "default to", "always do" |
   155	| `REINFORCEMENT` | Positive validation of existing spec-doc records | "that's right", "confirmed", "keep this" |
   156	| `DEPRECATION` | Marks memories as outdated | "no longer valid", "deprecated", "removed", "obsolete" |
   157	
   158	These signals are detected during `memory_match_triggers()` processing and influence save-time arbitration (prediction-error scoring) and correction tracking.
   159	
   160	### Trigger Sanitization (026 Remediation)
   161	
   162	Save-time trigger validation now applies sanitization rules to prevent low-quality triggers from degrading retrieval precision:
   163	
   164	| Rule | Behavior |
   165	|------|----------|
   166	| **Single-word filter** | Overly generic single-word triggers (e.g., "code", "fix", "error") are stripped during save-time validation to reduce false-positive surfacing |
   167	| **Duplicate detection** | Triggers that duplicate existing constitutional or high-importance memory triggers are flagged and removed |
   168	| **Length minimum** | Trigger phrases shorter than 3 characters are rejected |
   169	| **Stopword filter** | Common stopwords used alone as triggers are rejected |
   170

    21	import type {
    22	  MCPResponse,
    23	  SearchArgs,
    24	  TriggerArgs,
    25	  SaveArgs,
    26	  ListArgs,
    27	  StatsArgs,
    28	  HealthArgs,
    29	  DeleteArgs,

    34	
    35	function relabelResponseTool(response: MCPResponse, toolName: string): MCPResponse {
    36	  const firstEntry = response?.content?.[0];
    37	  if (!firstEntry || firstEntry.type !== 'text' || typeof firstEntry.text !== 'string') {
    38	    return response;
    39	  }
    40	
    41	  try {
    42	    const envelope = JSON.parse(firstEntry.text) as Record<string, unknown>;
    43	    const meta = envelope.meta && typeof envelope.meta === 'object'
    44	      ? envelope.meta as Record<string, unknown>
    45	      : {};
    46	    envelope.meta = {
    47	      ...meta,
    48	      tool: toolName,
    49	    };
    50	
    51	    return {
    52	      ...response,
    53	      content: [{ ...firstEntry, text: JSON.stringify(envelope, null, 2) }],
    54	    };
    55	  } catch {
    56	    return response;
    57	  }
    58	}
    59	
    60	/** Tool names handled by this module */

    62	  'memory_search',
    63	  'memory_quick_search',
    64	  'memory_match_triggers',
    65	  'memory_save',
    66	  'memory_list',
    67	  'memory_stats',
    68	  'memory_health',
    69	  'memory_delete',
    70	  'memory_update',
    71	  'memory_validate',
    72	  'memory_bulk_delete',
    73	]);
    74	
    75	/** Dispatch a tool call. Returns null if tool name not handled. */
    76	export async function handleTool(name: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<MCPResponse | null> {
    77	  switch (name) {
    78	    case 'memory_search':         return handleMemorySearch(parseArgs<SearchArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_search', args)));
    79	    case 'memory_quick_search': {
    80	      // E3: Delegate to memory_search with sensible defaults
    81	      const validated = validateToolArgs('memory_quick_search', args);
    82	      const quickArgs: SearchArgs = {
    83	        query: validated.query as string,
    84	        limit: typeof validated.limit === 'number' ? validated.limit : 10,
    85	        specFolder: validated.specFolder as string | undefined,
    86	        tenantId: validated.tenantId as string | undefined,
    87	        userId: validated.userId as string | undefined,
    88	        agentId: validated.agentId as string | undefined,
    89	        autoDetectIntent: true,
    90	        enableDedup: true,
    91	        includeContent: true,
    92	        includeConstitutional: true,
    93	        rerank: true,
    94	      };
    95	      const response = await handleMemorySearch(quickArgs);

codex
I found the current memory system surfaces. The short version: **Spec Kit Memory is packet-first now**. Saves no longer write active `specs/.../memory/*.md` notes; recovery goes through `handover.md -> _memory.continuity -> spec docs`, with indexed retrieval layered on top.

**Best Entry Points**
- [memory_system.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:17)  
  Main reference. Covers architecture, indexed sources, tiers, tools, search behavior.
- [save_workflow.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/references/memory/save_workflow.md:17)  
  Save path. `/memory:save` and direct script both route through `generate-context.js`.
- [trigger_config.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/references/memory/trigger_config.md:15)  
  Trigger phrases, Gate 3 trigger behavior, sanitization rules.
- [memory-tools.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tools/memory-tools.ts:60)  
  Live tool dispatcher for `memory_search`, `memory_quick_search`, `memory_match_triggers`, `memory_save`, CRUD, stats, health.

**Current Model**
- Canonical recovery chain: `handover.md -> _memory.continuity -> spec docs`.
- Indexed sources: `specs/**/*.md`, `.opencode/specs/**/*.md`, constitutional files, plus historical compatibility for old memory artifacts.
- Search is vector/FTS-backed with importance tiers; constitutional records always surface unless explicitly excluded.
- `memory_context()` is the preferred high-level retrieval entry point.
- `memory_search()` requires `query`, `concepts`, or cursor; `specFolder` alone is invalid.
- `memory_quick_search()` delegates to `memory_search` with defaults: content included, constitutional included, rerank enabled.
- Saves should use `/memory:save` or `node .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js ...`.

**Relevant Implementation Files**
- [memory-context.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts:1)
- [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts:1)
- [memory-save.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts:1)
- [resume-ladder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/resume/resume-ladder.ts)

One caveat: I attempted the live Spec Kit Memory MCP lookup, but that tool call was cancelled, so this pass is based on local repo evidence rather than the current memory DB contents.

**Current Model**

- Indexed sources: `specs/**/*.md`, `.opencode/specs/**/*.md`, constitutional files, plus historical compatibility for old memory artifacts.

- `memory_context()` is the preferred high-level retrieval entry point.

- `memory_quick_search()` delegates to `memory_search` with defaults: content included, constitutional included, rerank enabled.
- Saves should use `/memory:save` or `node .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js ...`.
